quick_skim.title: Pupil responses reveal temporally distinct signatures of value updating and decision strategy
quick_skim.abstract: Adaptive decision making depends on dopaminergic and noradrenergic systems supporting value learning and exploratory decision strategies, respectively, yet their contributions remain difficult to dissociate non-invasively. Here, we investigated whether pupil dynamics provide dissociable readouts of computational processes underlying value-based decision making across sensory modalities, potentially reflecting distinct neuromodulatory processes. Human participants performed a dynamic foraging task in which they chose between auditory, visual, or audio-visual options based on their estimated value, alongside a control task where choices were instructed. Behavior in the value-based task was well captured by a probabilistic choice model, revealing adaptive integration of reward history and a balance between exploration and exploitation. Pupil responses revealed temporally distinct computational signatures of decision strategy and value updating. Reaction time was associated with sustained pupil dilation throughout the decision process, whereas value differences between chosen and unchosen options selectively modulated pupil responses during stimulus evaluation and following feedback. These findings are consistent with computational processes linked to noradrenergic regulation of exploration-exploitation behavior and dopaminergic value updating, respectively. Both effects were significantly stronger during value-based than instructed decisions, indicating enhanced engagement of these computational processes when choices depended on learned reward values. Importantly, these effects were largely modality-independent, indicating a domain-general encoding of computational variables. Together, these findings identify pupil dynamics as a temporally sensitive and non-invasive marker of distinct computational stages underlying adaptive decision making and establish a framework for linking pupillometry to neuromodulatory theories of value learning and uncertainty processing.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: pupil dynamics and probabilistic choice model reveal decision strategies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 瞳孔动态和概率选择模型揭示决策策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 瞳孔动态和概率选择模型揭示决策策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 瞳孔动态和概率选择模型揭示决策策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This study uses pupil dynamics and a probabilistic choice model to dissociate value updating and decision strategy, providing a computational approach to infer internal cognitive processes from eye measures, relevant to understanding human goals via eye movements.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文利用瞳孔反应作为眼动信号，结合概率选择模型，来分离价值更新与决策策略的计算机制。通过动态觅食任务，发现瞳孔可以反映多巴胺能和去甲肾上腺素能系统的不同贡献。该方法为通过眼动数据理解人类目标与意图提供了计算框架，对实时意图识别有间接但重要的启示。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文利用瞳孔反应作为眼动信号，结合概率选择模型，来分离价值更新与决策策略的计算机制。通过动态觅食任务，发现瞳孔可以反映多巴胺能和去甲肾上腺素能系统的不同贡献。该方法为通过眼动数据理解人类目标与意图提供了计算框架，对实时意图识别有间接但重要的启示。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: computational models for understanding human goals through eye movements
